verdicts.reason: The option 'stackable_other_stackable_plugin_info' is only ever written in plugin.php lines 33-36, where it is populated exclusively from the PHP constants STACKABLE_BUILD and STACKABLE_VERSION. These constants are defined as hardcoded string literals ('free' and '3.19.9' respectively) on lines 45-46 of the same file. There is no code path that allows user-supplied or HTTP-request-derived data to reach this option. The taint analyzer incorrectly treated get_option() as a generic external source without tracking where the option value originates. No actual XSS risk exists.

manual_audit_notes: Plugin has 154 PHP files / 18237 lines. Additional manual spot-checks of output patterns found: (1) news.php feed output uses esc_url/esc_attr/esc_html throughout; (2) init.php json_encode for frontend vars is safe; (3) css-optimize.php echoes CSS through apply_filters which is not user-direct; (4) welcome/index.php admin_url() output is safe. No AJAX handlers, REST routes, shortcodes, file uploads, or direct DB queries were found by the pipeline — consistent with this being a block library whose attack surface is almost entirely block rendering in JS/block.json. No confirmed vulnerabilities.
